evals.name: git-hung-check
evals.prompt: check if my git is hung
evals.expected_output: A diagnostic plan that uses macOS-compatible timeouts, wraps git network probes (ls-remote, fetch) in a timeout, parses git process elapsed time numerically (not string compare), and produces a P0/P1/P2/P3 punch list separating auto-fixes from user actions. Describe commands; do not actually run state-changing ones.

assertions.text: Output uses a macOS-compatible timeout pattern (run_with_timeout helper, gtimeout fallback, or shell-native background-PID-and-kill), NOT bare GNU `timeout`.
Output wraps git ls-remote and git fetch (--dry-run) calls in a timeout.
Output parses git process elapsed time (etime) into numeric seconds before thresholding, not via string comparison on hh:mm:ss.
Output produces a P0/P1/P2/P3 punch list that separates auto-fixed items from user-action items.

evals.name: docker-hanging-check
evals.expected_output: A diagnostic plan that probes the docker daemon using a macOS-compatible timeout helper (not bare `timeout`), wraps every docker probe so a wedged daemon cannot hang the run, and produces a P0/P1/P2/P3 punch list separating auto-fixes from user actions. Describe commands; do not actually run state-changing ones.

assertions.text: Output uses a macOS-compatible timeout pattern (run_with_timeout helper, gtimeout fallback, or shell-native background-PID-and-kill), NOT bare GNU `timeout`.
Output wraps git ls-remote and git fetch (--dry-run) calls in a timeout (when git checks are run as part of the broader sweep).
Output parses process elapsed time (etime) into numeric seconds before thresholding, not via string comparison on hh:mm:ss.
Output produces a P0/P1/P2/P3 punch list that separates auto-fixed items from user-action items.

evals.name: full-env-health-check
evals.expected_output: A complete diagnostic sweep across git, docker, Xcode, MCP servers, disk, and plugin loading. Uses a macOS-compatible timeout helper (not bare `timeout`), wraps every external probe (docker, git ls-remote, git fetch, git fsck) in the helper, parses git process elapsed time numerically, and ends with a P0/P1/P2/P3 punch list separating auto-fixes from user actions. Describe commands; do not actually run state-changing ones.

assertions.text: Output uses a macOS-compatible timeout pattern (run_with_timeout helper, gtimeout fallback, or shell-native background-PID-and-kill), NOT bare GNU `timeout`.
Output wraps git ls-remote and git fetch (--dry-run) calls in a timeout.
Output parses git process elapsed time (etime) into numeric seconds before thresholding, not via string comparison on hh:mm:ss.
Output produces a P0/P1/P2/P3 punch list that separates auto-fixed items from user-action items.
